identificationModule.officialTitle: Healthy Minds Program (HMP) App Dosage Study

collaborators.name: National Center for Complementary and Integrative Health (NCCIH)

descriptionModule.briefSummary: The study will evaluate the effectiveness of the Healthy Minds Program (HMP) meditation app on participants experiencing depression and/or anxiety. Participants will be assigned to use the HMP app 5- or 15-minutes per day for 4 weeks.
descriptionModule.detailedDescription: Depression and anxiety are highly common, but access to evidence-based treatments is limited. Meditation training delivered through mobile health (mHealth) technology may increase access to effective strategies. However, the necessary dosage of meditation practice to produce benefits is unknown. This project will investigate the feasibility, acceptability, and preliminary effectiveness of the HMP app for depression and/or anxiety and the feasibility of randomly assigning participants to practice dosages. Further, the project will provide a preliminary evaluation of the linkages between practice dosage and outcomes assessed retrospectively and in-the-moment via ecological momentary assessment (EMA).

designInfo.interventionModelDescription: Participants will be randomized to two groups in a 1:1 ratio

armGroups.label: 5-minute app usage
armGroups.description: Participants will be asked to use the HMP app for 5-minutes per day.

armGroups.label: 15-minute app usage
armGroups.description: Participants will be asked to use the HMP app for 15-minutes per day.

interventions.name: HMP app
interventions.description: The HMP app is a meditation-based smartphone app designed to promote and protect psychological well-being through sustainable skills training. The program is grounded in constituents of psychological well-being identified in empirical literature. HMP provides core content, with instruction administered through a curriculum of guided practices. HMP has guided audio practices that address 4 constituents of well-being: awareness, connection, insight, and purpose.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Randomization Rate: Number of Participants Who Were Randomized and Completed EMA Assessments and Activities in the HMP App
primaryOutcomes.description: Percentage of those randomized completing ecological momentary assessment (EMA) assessments and activities in the Healthy Minds Program (HMP) app

primaryOutcomes.measure: Dosage Adherence: Number of Participants Adhering to Their Dosage Greater Than or Equal to 50 Percent of the Days
primaryOutcomes.description: Number of participants adhering to their assigned dosage greater than or equal to 50 percent of the days. This was assessed directly through the HMP app.
Percentage of EMA assessments that were completed
Percentage of participants completing pre- and post-test measures

primaryOutcomes.measure: Number of Participants Completing Practices ≥50% of Days
primaryOutcomes.description: Number of participants completing practices ≥50% of days

The MSUS is a 10-item questionnaire where participants report their experience of the usability of a product, in this case the HMP app. It is scored on a 1- to 5-point Likert scale where 1 = strongly disagree and 5 = strongly agree. The result was rescaled to range from 0 to 100 with higher scores indicate higher usability.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Change in Attitudes Towards Psychological Online Interventions (ATPOI)
primaryOutcomes.description: The ATPOI is a 16-item questionnaire where participants report their perception of psychological online interventions. It is scored on a 1- to 5-point Likert scale where 1 = totally disagree and 5 = totally agree. Mean scores are reported for a total possible range of scores is 1 to 5 where higher scores indicate more positive attitudes towards psychological online interventions.
Number of days the app was accessed.

primaryOutcomes.measure: HMP App Utilization: Number of Activities

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in Composite of Patient-Reported Outcomes Measurement Information System (PROMIS) Depression and PROMIS Anxiety
secondaryOutcomes.description: Psychological distress will be computed as a composite of the PROMIS Depression and PROMIS Anxiety measures. We will use the computer adaptive version of both measures which have a mean of 50 and a standard deviation of 10. A composite will be computed by average across T-scores. T-scores range from 10 to 90 with higher scores indicating higher depression or anxiety.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in Healthy Minds Index (HM Index): Awareness
secondaryOutcomes.description: The HM Index assesses qualities trained in the HMP app (awareness, connection, insight, purpose). It is scored on a 0- to 4-point Likert scale where 0 = a low amount (e.g., never, not at all, none of the time) and 4 = a higher amount (e.g., always, to the highest degree, all of the time) of a particular quality. There are 4 awareness items. Mean item scores are reported with a range from 0 to 4 where higher scores indicate more awareness.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in Healthy Minds Index (HM Index): Connection
secondaryOutcomes.description: The HM Index assesses qualities trained in the HMP app (awareness, connection, insight, purpose). It is scored on a 0- to 4-point Likert scale where 0 = a low amount (e.g., never, not at all, none of the time) and 4 = a higher amount (e.g., always, to the highest degree, all of the time) of a particular quality. There are 6 connection items. Mean item scores are reported with a range from 0 to 4 where higher scores indicate more connection.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in Healthy Minds Index (HM Index): Insight
secondaryOutcomes.description: The HM Index assesses qualities trained in the HMP app (awareness, connection, insight, purpose). It is scored on a 0- to 4-point Likert scale where 0 = a low amount (e.g., never, not at all, none of the time) and 4 = a higher amount (e.g., always, to the highest degree, all of the time) of a particular quality. There are 3 insight items. Mean item scores are reported with a range from 0 to 4 where higher scores indicate more insight.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in Healthy Minds Index (HM Index): Purpose
secondaryOutcomes.description: The HM Index assesses qualities trained in the HMP app (awareness, connection, insight, purpose). It is scored on a 0- to 4-point Likert scale where 0 = a low amount (e.g., never, not at all, none of the time) and 4 = a higher amount (e.g., always, to the highest degree, all of the time) of a particular quality. There are 4 purpose items. Mean item scores are reported with a range from 0 to 4 where higher scores indicate more purpose.

Well-being Growth Mindset is a 3-item questionnaire assessing participants' perception of the malleability of wellbeing. It is scored on a 1- to 6-point Likert scale where 1 = strongly disagree and 6 = strongly agree. Total scores range from 3 to 18 where higher scores indicate more of a growth mindset (i.e., wellbeing is malleable).

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in Five Facet Mindfulness Questionnaire (FFMQ) Awareness Subscale
secondaryOutcomes.description: FFMQ Awareness is a 8-item questionnaire assessing mindful awareness. It is scored on a 1- to 5-point Likert scale where 1 = never or very rarely true and 5 = very often or always true. Mean scores are reported with a range from 1 to 5 where higher scores indicate more mindful awareness.

NIH Toolbox Loneliness is a 5-item questionnaire assessing loneliness. It is scored on a 1- to 5-point Likert scale where 1 = never and 5 = always. Mean scores are reported for a range from 1 to 5 where higher scores indicate more loneliness.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in Experiences Questionnaire (EQ) Decentering Subscale
secondaryOutcomes.description: Experiences Questionnaire Decentering is an 11-item questionnaire assessing the psychological capacity of decentering. It is scored on a 1- to 5-point Likert scale where 1 = never and 5 = all the time. Mean scores are reported for a total range from 1 to 5 where higher scores indicate more decentering.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in Meaning in Life Questionnaire (MLQ) Presence Subscale
secondaryOutcomes.description: MLQ Presence is a 5-item questionnaire assessing the presence of meaning in life. It is scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = absolutely untrue and 7 = absolutely true. Mean scores are reported for a total range from 1 to 7 where higher scores indicate greater presence of meaning in life.

Sleep disturbance will be assessed using the computer adaptive version of the PROMIS Sleep Disturbance measure. This measure produces scores a T-score with a mean of 50 and a standard deviation of 10. Higher scores indicate greater sleep disturbance.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Count of Participant Responses to Adverse Event Relevant Survey Item
secondaryOutcomes.description: Participants were asked a single survey item to assess their meditation experience. The query was "I had challenging, difficult, or distressing experiences as a result of my meditation during this study." Participants chose from the following responses: Never, Rarely, Occasionally, Regularly, or Frequently. Count of participants in each arm answering each response is reported here.

otherOutcomes.measure: Change in EMA-assessed Psychological Distress (Composite of Depression and Anxiety Items)
otherOutcomes.description: A single item reflecting depression and a single item reflecting anxiety are scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all and 7 = very much.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: 4 times per day from baseline to post-test (week 4)

otherOutcomes.measure: Change in EMA-assessed Mindfulness of Activities
otherOutcomes.description: A single item mindfulness of activities is scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all and 7 = very much.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: 4 times per day from baseline to post-test (week 4)

otherOutcomes.measure: Change in EMA-assessed Mindfulness of Feelings / Emotions
otherOutcomes.description: A single item assessing awareness of feelings is scored on a 1- to 5-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all aware and 5 = extremely aware.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: 4 times per day from baseline to post-test (week 4)

otherOutcomes.description: A single item assessing loneliness is scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all and 7 = very much.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: 4 times per day from baseline to post-test (week 4)

otherOutcomes.measure: Change in EMA-assessed Connection With Others
otherOutcomes.description: A single item assessing connection with others is scored on a 1- to 5-point Likert scale where 1 = not often at all and 5 = extremely often
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: 4 times per day from baseline to post-test (week 4)

otherOutcomes.description: A single item assessing decentering (i.e., experiencing thoughts more as events than direct reflection of reality) is scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all and 7 = very much.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: 4 times per day from baseline to post-test (week 4)

otherOutcomes.description: A single item assessing insight is scored on a 1- to 5-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all aware and 5 = extremely aware
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: 4 times per day from baseline to post-test (week 4)

otherOutcomes.description: A single item assessing purpose is scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all and 7 = very much
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: 4 times per day from baseline to post-test (week 4)

otherOutcomes.description: A single item assessing stressor exposure is scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all and 7 = very much.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: 4 times per day from baseline to post-test (week 4)

otherOutcomes.measure: Change in EMA-assessed Personal Values / Meaning
otherOutcomes.description: A single item assessing reflection of personal values in current activity is scored on a 1- to 5-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all reflected and 5 = extremely reflected.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: 4 times per day from baseline to post-test (week 4)

otherOutcomes.description: A single item assessing rumination is scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all and 7 = very much
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: 4 times per day from baseline to post-test (week 4)

otherOutcomes.description: A single item assessing application of meditation techniques in daily life (informal practice) is scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all and 7 = very much.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: 4 times per day from baseline to post-test (week 4)

otherOutcomes.measure: Change in Pre- and Post-practice Positive Affect
otherOutcomes.description: Two items assessing positive affect are scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all and 7 = very much.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: Before and after meditation practices between baseline and post-test (week 4)

otherOutcomes.measure: Change in Pre- and Post-practice Negative Affect
otherOutcomes.description: Two items assessing negative affect are scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all and 7 = very much
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: Before and after meditation practices between baseline and post-test (week 4)

otherOutcomes.description: A single item assessing focus during meditation is scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all and 7 = most of the time
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: Before and after meditation practices between baseline and post-test (week 4)

otherOutcomes.description: The DWAI is a 6-item questionnaire assessing participants' alliance with the HMP app. It is scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = strongly disagree and 7 = strongly agree. Total scores range from 6 to 42 where higher scores indicate a stronger working alliance.

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Age 18 or older
* Access to a smartphone or device capable of downloading HMP app
* Self-reported willingness to complete 1-2 min survey 4 times per day on phone for 4 weeks
* Able to speak, read, and write in English
* PROMIS Depression or PROMIS Anxiety \>55

Exclusion Criteria:

* Meditation retreat experience
* Regular meditation practice (weekly practice for over 1 year OR daily practice within the previous 6 months)
* Previous practice under the instruction of a meditation teacher, other than in the context of an introductory course
* PROMIS Depression \>70
* AUDIT C score greater than or equal to 4 for men, and greater than or equal to 3 for non-men (any other genders)

references.citation: Dyer RL, Zimmerman KM, Zhao X, Xie Q, Dahl CJ, Quanbeck A, Goldberg SB. Developing meditation practice in individuals with elevated psychological distress via a meditation app intervention: An implementation science-informed qualitative investigation of barriers and facilitators. Psychol Serv. 2025 Jul 10:10.1037/ser0000980. doi: 10.1037/ser0000980. Online ahead of print.

Xie Q, Dyer RL, Lam SU, Frye C, Dahl CJ, Quanbeck A, Nahum-Shani I, Davidson RJ, Goldberg SB. Understanding the Implementation of Informal Meditation Practice in a Smartphone-Based Intervention: A Qualitative Analysis. Mindfulness (N Y). 2024 Feb;15(2):479-490. doi: 10.1007/s12671-024-02304-x. Epub 2024 Jan 22.

ipdSharingStatementModule.description: Data will be de-identified and listed in public data registries (e.g., OSF) based on the publication of preregistered study hypotheses on a manuscript by manuscript basis.
ipdSharingStatementModule.timeFrame: Data may be shared following analysis and publication or upon request.
ipdSharingStatementModule.accessCriteria: Researcher consent. Any reasonable request will be granted.

participantFlowModule.recruitmentDetails: Participants were enrolled from February 2022 to June 2022.

outcomeMeasures.title: Change in Healthy Minds Index (HM Index): Connection
outcomeMeasures.description: The HM Index assesses qualities trained in the HMP app (awareness, connection, insight, purpose). It is scored on a 0- to 4-point Likert scale where 0 = a low amount (e.g., never, not at all, none of the time) and 4 = a higher amount (e.g., always, to the highest degree, all of the time) of a particular quality. There are 6 connection items. Mean item scores are reported with a range from 0 to 4 where higher scores indicate more connection.

groups.title: 5-minute App Usage
groups.description:
Participants will be asked to use the HMP app for 5-minutes per day.

The HMP app is a meditation-based smartphone app designed to promote and protect psychological well-being through sustainable skills training. HMP provides core content, with instruction administered through a curriculum of guided practices. HMP has guided audio practices that address 4 constituents of well-being: awareness, connection, insight, and purpose.

outcomeMeasures.title: Change in Wellbeing Growth Mindset
outcomeMeasures.description: Well-being Growth Mindset is a 3-item questionnaire assessing participants' perception of the malleability of wellbeing. It is scored on a 1- to 6-point Likert scale where 1 = strongly disagree and 6 = strongly agree. Total scores range from 3 to 18 where higher scores indicate more of a growth mindset (i.e., wellbeing is malleable).

groups.description:

outcomeMeasures.title: Change in Five Facet Mindfulness Questionnaire (FFMQ) Awareness Subscale
outcomeMeasures.description: FFMQ Awareness is a 8-item questionnaire assessing mindful awareness. It is scored on a 1- to 5-point Likert scale where 1 = never or very rarely true and 5 = very often or always true. Mean scores are reported with a range from 1 to 5 where higher scores indicate more mindful awareness.

outcomeMeasures.title: Change in NIH Toolbox Loneliness
outcomeMeasures.description: NIH Toolbox Loneliness is a 5-item questionnaire assessing loneliness. It is scored on a 1- to 5-point Likert scale where 1 = never and 5 = always. Mean scores are reported for a range from 1 to 5 where higher scores indicate more loneliness.

groups.description:

outcomeMeasures.title: Change in Experiences Questionnaire (EQ) Decentering Subscale
outcomeMeasures.description: Experiences Questionnaire Decentering is an 11-item questionnaire assessing the psychological capacity of decentering. It is scored on a 1- to 5-point Likert scale where 1 = never and 5 = all the time. Mean scores are reported for a total range from 1 to 5 where higher scores indicate more decentering.

outcomeMeasures.title: Change in Meaning in Life Questionnaire (MLQ) Presence Subscale
outcomeMeasures.description: MLQ Presence is a 5-item questionnaire assessing the presence of meaning in life. It is scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = absolutely untrue and 7 = absolutely true. Mean scores are reported for a total range from 1 to 7 where higher scores indicate greater presence of meaning in life.

outcomeMeasures.title: Change in PROMIS Sleep Disturbance
outcomeMeasures.description: Sleep disturbance will be assessed using the computer adaptive version of the PROMIS Sleep Disturbance measure. This measure produces scores a T-score with a mean of 50 and a standard deviation of 10. Higher scores indicate greater sleep disturbance.

groups.title: 5-minute App Usage
groups.description:
Participants will be asked to use the HMP app for 5-minutes per day.

outcomeMeasures.title: Count of Participant Responses to Adverse Event Relevant Survey Item
outcomeMeasures.description: Participants were asked a single survey item to assess their meditation experience. The query was "I had challenging, difficult, or distressing experiences as a result of my meditation during this study." Participants chose from the following responses: Never, Rarely, Occasionally, Regularly, or Frequently. Count of participants in each arm answering each response is reported here.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: 3 participants withdrew from the study prior to the post-test measure, 1 participant did not answer the question

groups.title: 5-minute App Usage
groups.description:
Participants will be asked to use the HMP app for 5-minutes per day.

The HMP app is a meditation-based smartphone app designed to promote and protect psychological well-being through sustainable skills training. The program is grounded in constituents of psychological well-being identified in empirical literature. HMP provides core content, with instruction administered through a curriculum of guided practices. HMP has guided audio practices that address 4 constituents of well-being: awareness, connection, insight, and purpose.

groups.title: 15-minute App Usage
groups.description:

The program is grounded in constituents of psychological well-being identified in empirical literature. HMP provides core content, with instruction administered through a curriculum of guided practices. outcomeMeasures.title: Change in EMA-assessed Psychological Distress (Composite of Depression and Anxiety Items)
outcomeMeasures.description: A single item reflecting depression and a single item reflecting anxiety are scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all and 7 = very much.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: 4 times per day from baseline to post-test (week 4)

outcomeMeasures.title: Change in EMA-assessed Mindfulness of Activities
outcomeMeasures.description: A single item mindfulness of activities is scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all and 7 = very much.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: 4 times per day from baseline to post-test (week 4)

outcomeMeasures.title: Change in EMA-assessed Mindfulness of Feelings / Emotions
outcomeMeasures.description: A single item assessing awareness of feelings is scored on a 1- to 5-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all aware and 5 = extremely aware.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: 4 times per day from baseline to post-test (week 4)

outcomeMeasures.title: Change in EMA-assessed Loneliness
outcomeMeasures.description: A single item assessing loneliness is scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all and 7 = very much.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: 4 times per day from baseline to post-test (week 4)

outcomeMeasures.title: Change in EMA-assessed Connection With Others
outcomeMeasures.description: A single item assessing connection with others is scored on a 1- to 5-point Likert scale where 1 = not often at all and 5 = extremely often
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: 4 times per day from baseline to post-test (week 4)

outcomeMeasures.title: Change in EMA-assessed Decentering
outcomeMeasures.description: A single item assessing decentering (i.e., experiencing thoughts more as events than direct reflection of reality) is scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all and 7 = very much.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: 4 times per day from baseline to post-test (week 4)

outcomeMeasures.title: Change in EMA-assessed Insight
outcomeMeasures.description: A single item assessing insight is scored on a 1- to 5-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all aware and 5 = extremely aware
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: 4 times per day from baseline to post-test (week 4)

outcomeMeasures.title: Change in EMA-assessed Purpose
outcomeMeasures.description: A single item assessing purpose is scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all and 7 = very much
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: 4 times per day from baseline to post-test (week 4)

outcomeMeasures.title: Change in EMA-assessed Stressor Exposure
outcomeMeasures.description: A single item assessing stressor exposure is scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all and 7 = very much.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: 4 times per day from baseline to post-test (week 4)

outcomeMeasures.title: Change in EMA-assessed Personal Values / Meaning
outcomeMeasures.description: A single item assessing reflection of personal values in current activity is scored on a 1- to 5-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all reflected and 5 = extremely reflected.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: 4 times per day from baseline to post-test (week 4)

outcomeMeasures.title: Change in EMA-assessed Rumination
outcomeMeasures.description: A single item assessing rumination is scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all and 7 = very much
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: 4 times per day from baseline to post-test (week 4)

outcomeMeasures.title: Change in EMA-assessed Informal Practice
outcomeMeasures.description: A single item assessing application of meditation techniques in daily life (informal practice) is scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all and 7 = very much.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: 4 times per day from baseline to post-test (week 4)

outcomeMeasures.title: Change in Pre- and Post-practice Positive Affect
outcomeMeasures.description: Two items assessing positive affect are scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all and 7 = very much.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: Before and after meditation practices between baseline and post-test (week 4)

outcomeMeasures.title: Change in Pre- and Post-practice Negative Affect
outcomeMeasures.description: Two items assessing negative affect are scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all and 7 = very much
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: Before and after meditation practices between baseline and post-test (week 4)

outcomeMeasures.title: Change in Post-practice Focus
outcomeMeasures.description: A single item assessing focus during meditation is scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = not at all and 7 = most of the time
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: Before and after meditation practices between baseline and post-test (week 4)

outcomeMeasures.title: Digital Working Alliance Inventory (DWAI)
outcomeMeasures.description: The DWAI is a 6-item questionnaire assessing participants' alliance with the HMP app. It is scored on a 1- to 7-point Likert scale where 1 = strongly disagree and 7 = strongly agree. Total scores range from 6 to 42 where higher scores indicate a stronger working alliance.

adverseEventsModule.description: A single survey item was asked of participants "I had challenging, difficult, or distressing experiences as a result of my meditation during this study". Participants could answer Never, Rarely, Occasionally, Regularly, or Frequently. Please see Secondary Outcome Measures for count of participant responses.

eventGroups.title: 5-minute App Usage
eventGroups.description:
Participants will be asked to use the HMP app for 5-minutes per day.

The HMP app is a meditation-based smartphone app designed to promote and protect psychological well-being through sustainable skills training. The program is grounded in constituents of psychological well-being identified in empirical literature. HMP provides core content, with instruction administered through a curriculum of guided practices. HMP has guided audio practices that address 4 constituents of well-being: awareness, connection, insight, and purpose.

eventGroups.title: 15-minute App Usage
eventGroups.description:

HMP has guided audio practices that address 4 constituents of well-being: awareness, connection, insight, and purpose.

limitationsAndCaveats.description: This study was not powered to detect between-group differences on pre-post outcomes. Here results are reported by arm for descriptive purposes.

pointOfContact.title: Simon B. Goldberg, PhD
pointOfContact.organization: Center for Healthy Minds, University of Wisconsin - Madison
